evals.prompt: Design the instruction-file architecture for a repo that supports Copilot, Gemini, and Claude without duplicating the same workflow text in three places.
evals.expected_output: Guidance that chooses one source of truth, recommends thin provider bridges, and separates durable repo rules from domain-specific skill detail.
assertions: The response chooses one instruction source of truth
The response recommends thin bridge or stub files for the other providers
The response distinguishes top-level instruction content from skill-level detail

evals.prompt: Review .github/copilot-instructions.md after several new skills were added and tell me what should change.
evals.expected_output: A review that updates the skill list, routing hints, and any workflow commands that changed, while keeping feature-level detail in skills.
assertions: The response checks skill listings or routing hints
The response checks workflow or command drift
The response avoids moving feature-level detail into the top-level file by default

evals.prompt: Review GEMINI.md and tell me whether it is too heavy or whether it should just bridge back to the shared instructions.
evals.expected_output: Guidance that keeps GEMINI.md thin by default, allows only narrow Gemini-specific notes, and routes back to the shared source of truth.
assertions: The response keeps GEMINI.md thin by default
The response allows only narrow provider-specific exceptions
The response routes Gemini back to the shared instructions

evals.prompt: Review .claude/CLAUDE.md and tell me whether it is too heavy or whether it should just bridge back to the shared instructions.
evals.expected_output: Guidance that keeps .claude/CLAUDE.md thin by default, allows only narrow Claude-specific notes, and routes back to the shared source of truth.
assertions: The response keeps .claude/CLAUDE.md thin by default
The response allows only narrow provider-specific exceptions
The response routes Claude back to the shared instructions
